item.title: The Rise of the House Manager
item.description: More families who can afford it are hiring a “chief of staff for the home.”
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: Elon Musk Is Taking the X Playbook to Starlink
item.description: The world’s richest man is accruing more power than ever before.
item.dc:creator: Quinn Slobodian

item.title: DefineJewish State
item.description: The phrase is even more complicated than it appears.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: Big Sky Crack-Up
item.description: Montana Democrats thought they found a novel way to win control of the U.S. Senate—until the party faithful started fighting back.
item.dc:creator: Michael Scherer

item.title: The Rise of CliffsNotes Cinema
item.description: Oversimplified literary remakes miss the point of the works they are adapting.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Deadly Sins
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Why Justin Bieber Played YouTube Onstage for Thousands of People
item.description: The pop star transformed the normal act of browsing your laptop into something interesting—and unsettling.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kornhaber

item.title: Peter Hujar’s Photos Are All the Rage. He’d Be Shocked.
item.description: A new biography brings the the late photographer’s relationship with the artist Paul Thek to vivid life.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ferri

item.title: An Extra-Embarrassing White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: Why is Donald Trump breaking bread with the “enemy of the people”?
item.dc:creator: Paul Farhi

item.title: The New Age of Performance Anxiety
item.description: With the rise of screen culture, all the world has stage fright.
item.dc:creator: Megan Garber

item.title: On Losing a Daughter
item.description: The people we were died at the exact moment our child did.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Crittenden

item.title: Robert Rauschenberg’s Penchant for Invention and Spectacle
item.description: In 2007, he and Merce Cunningham put a new twist on a famous 1981 sculpture.
item.dc:creator: Amy Weiss-Meyer

item.title: Have Accommodations Become an Unfair Advantage in Higher Education?
item.description: Readers respond to “Accommodation Nation”

item.title: The Aides Keeping the President in the Dark
item.description: Donald Trump’s advisers are treating him like he can’t handle the reality of the war in Iran. They might be right—but that fact is a danger to the constitutional order.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The End of the Argumentad Orbánum
item.description: America is more resilient than the pessimists think.
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: San Francisco Solved Metro Vandalism With One Neat Trick
item.description: The age of the fare-gate society is here.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: DOJ’s First ‘Weaponization’ Report Is a Bust
item.description: The document purports to show bias under the Biden administration—and fails spectacularly.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: The Most Tortured Relationship in America
item.description: Monogamy is one of the last bipartisan ideals—even if people struggle to live up to it.
item.dc:creator: Faith Hill

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Free Bread
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Where Did ‘Let Them’ Come From?
item.description: Years before Mel Robbins published her best-selling self-help book, a struggling writer posted a poem with a similar message.
item.dc:creator: Olga Khazan
